human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for hospital bed-management specificity, source quality, ADT and bed-board truth sets, capacity and allocation boundaries, clinical-versus-administrative separation, PHI controls, payer and utilization dependencies, scenario coverage, commercialization notes, and validated completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to hospital bed management for ADT, EHR encounter, bed board, capacity dashboard, staffed-bed roster, EVS, transport, isolation, transfer, discharge, authorization, and approval evidence and does not claim clinical, payer, privacy, legal, staffing, transfer, or final approval authority.

Authority sources cover CMS hospital CoPs, ADT notification operating guidance, discharge planning, utilization review, nursing services, EMTALA-sensitive capacity, HHS OCR minimum-necessary PHI handling, CDC patient placement, AHRQ patient flow, and HL7 location and encounter terminology.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal placement recommendations, capacity overload, missing tie-break rules, final approval refusal, missing authorization/referral/eligibility dependencies, PHI-sensitive census requests, clinical boundary cases, infection-control uncertainty, system conflicts, transfer-center boundaries, generic facilities rejection, low-confidence missing tenant policy, conflicting policies, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/hospitals-and-health-systems/bed-management-specialist/ so hospital bed management remains separated from generic healthcare operations, facilities scheduling, clinical review, utilization management, discharge planning, transfer-center legal decisions, privacy, and revenue cycle ownership.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.

Boundary enforcement remained strong around clinical acuity, level of care, discharge readiness, infection-control clearance, utilization review, payer interpretation, PHI disclosure, transfer acceptance, staffing adequacy, and final operational approval.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated hospitals and health systems bed management specialist that supports evidence-backed bed demand, capacity, sequencing, readiness, and escalation control.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
